(2017) for horizon-invariant constraint enforcement and Chen et al. (2025) for the augmented Lagrangian formulation; verifying existence, year, and attributed content is a plausible primary focus of 'check this'."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus primarily on verifying that the named citations (Achiam et al. 2017; Chen et al. 2025) accurately describe what the text attributes to them, or on auditing whether the quantitative claims and architectural conclusions follow logically from the described experiments?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains quantitative ablation results into architectural conclusions (dispatcher vs. Lagrangian multiplier); verifying that the causal logic of those argument steps is sound is a central plausible target of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.3: Five specific numeric claims are asserted (30.7 pp QoS, 16.1% cost reduction, 4.0 pp QoS, 44.1 pp commitment, 55% baseline) without visible supporting tables or figures in the excerpt; checking whether stated numbers are grounded in reported evidence is centrally at risk.
rationale.R1.7: Two named citations are used as load-bearing theoretical grounding — Achiam et al. (2017) for horizon-invariant constraint enforcement and Chen et al. (2025) for the augmented Lagrangian formulation; verifying existence, year, and attributed content is a plausible primary focus of 'check this'.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on verifying that the named citations (Achiam et al. 2017; Chen et al. 2025) accurately describe what the text attributes to them, or on auditing whether the quantitative claims and architectural conclusions follow logically from the described experiments?
